summary: Well-scoped GPU migration of an existing CPU animation pipeline. The plan faithfully ports all six motion effects (drift, jitter, breathing, bass expansion, treble sparkle, pointer repulsion) from the per-point JS loop to a custom ShaderMaterial, adds bass twist and time-evolution as new warp dimensions, and introduces no breaking changes to the GeometrySystem interface or render loop contract. The blast radius is contained to one modified file and three new files.

architectural_impact.public_api_changes: None. PointCloud still implements GeometrySystem { init(), draw(), cleanup() }. The positions getter continues to return basePositions (pre-deformation), which is correct since GPU deformation is invisible to JS.
architectural_impact.dependency_changes: None. No new npm packages. GLSL imports use Vite built-in ?raw suffix.
architectural_impact.build_pipeline_changes: Minor: a new .d.ts declaration for '*.glsl?raw' modules is needed. No Vite plugin required.

strengths: Correct identification that the per-point for-loop (lines 174-218 of pointCloud.ts) is the CPU bottleneck — moving 1200×6 trig calls per frame to GPU is the right architectural move
Layered sine noise avoids a noise texture or external GLSL library while staying within GLSL ES 1.0 compatibility — good portability choice
Uniform-only draw() updates (O(1) per frame instead of O(n)) preserves the render loop's frame budget for other future geometry systems
Plan preserves mesh-level rotation on CPU (single matrix op) rather than over-engineering it into the shader — correct separation of concerns
Acceptance criteria mapping is explicit and traceable: each criterion maps to specific shader functions
Test plan (20 cases) covers uniforms, attributes, determinism, cleanup, privacy, and the critical 'no CPU buffer writes' assertion (T-032-11)
The plan correctly keeps basePositions for the public positions getter, maintaining API compatibility

concerns.detail: The plan lists 'color' as an existing attribute to keep, but the vertex shader computes vColor from aHueOffset + uPaletteHue + treble modulation. The per-point color attribute (Float32Array of effectiveCount*3) would be uploaded but never read by the shader. Either remove it from geometry or use it as a fallback. This is a minor memory waste (~14KB at 1200 points), not a correctness issue.
concerns.recommendation: Remove the color attribute from geometry since the shader computes color from aHueOffset and uniforms. Update init() accordingly.

concerns.detail: The current PointsMaterial uses a uniform size (0.06 * factor) and ignores the per-point 'size' BufferAttribute. The plan's vertex shader reads 'size' as an attribute for gl_PointSize. This is correct behavior and actually improves on the current state — but the plan should be explicit that per-point size variation now actually takes effect (it was silently ignored before).
concerns.recommendation: No action needed — this is an improvement. Just be aware in visual testing that point size distribution will change slightly.

concerns.area: HSL to RGB conversion in GLSL
concerns.detail: The vertex shader needs to convert HSL (hue from aHueOffset + uPaletteHue, saturation from uPaletteSaturation, lightness from treble modulation) to RGB for vColor. The plan's shader design section doesn't mention an HSL-to-RGB function, but one is required since GLSL has no built-in HSL support. This is ~10 lines of GLSL and well-known, but it's an unstated dependency.
concerns.recommendation: Add an hsl2rgb helper function to the vertex shader. Standard implementation using the 6-sector piecewise formula.

concerns.detail: The plan mentions 'screen-projected space' for pointer repulsion, but uPointerPos is passed as normalized [0,1] coordinates. The vertex shader operates in model/world space. The current CPU implementation (lines 193-202) approximates this by dividing base position by 3 to get a [-0.67, 0.67] range comparable to pointer's [-0.5, 0.5]. The GPU version needs the same approximation or a proper model-to-screen projection, which isn't detailed in the shader design.
concerns.recommendation: Port the same approximation (position.xy / 3.0 - uPointerPos) to the vertex shader. Don't over-engineer a full projection matrix approach.

concerns.detail: The project has no vite.config.ts. The ?raw suffix works without configuration in Vite, so no config file needs to be created. Only the TypeScript declaration (*.glsl?raw → string) is needed.
concerns.recommendation: Add the declaration to src/vite-env.d.ts (create it) or a src/shaders.d.ts file as the plan suggests.

missing_from_plan: HSL-to-RGB conversion function in the vertex shader (required but not mentioned)
Explicit removal or repurposing of the now-unused color attribute from BufferGeometry
The sparkle effect gating (enableSparkle config option) — the plan doesn't mention preserving the enableSparkle flag from PointCloudConfig; currently it gates treble-driven lightness modulation

gaps: No test for HSL-to-RGB correctness (acceptable — this is a well-known GLSL function)
No test verifying enableSparkle config flag is respected in the shader path
T-032-11 (no CPU buffer writes) is the most architecturally important test — good that it's included

recommendation: Proceed with implementation. Address the HSL-to-RGB helper and color attribute cleanup during step 1 and step 3 respectively. The plan is architecturally sound and well-contained.
